note: Field converged on a common shape (agentic loop + MCP + subagents + hooks + skills + git worktrees + parallel/cloud exec), so raw feature parity is table stakes. Vendor claims are product signals, not neutral benchmarks. Claude Code studied deepest.

competitors.name: Claude Code
competitors.chat_ux: Terminal REPL; streamed gather->act->verify; Esc interrupt-and-keep + soft steer; unified / palette; Ctrl+T todos; Ctrl+O transcript; scriptable status line; whimsical spinner
competitors.ide_ux: Native VS Code/Cursor extension (editable plan doc, inline diffs, @file#L, parallel tabs, Remote tab); JetBrains beta (terminal + getDiagnostics only); Apr-14 desktop redesign added a multi-agent session sidebar [verifier: 'no GUI' is OUTDATED]
competitors.agent_loop: Flat gather-act-verify; /goal = separate Haiku evaluator (transcript-only) drives to a completion condition; /loop; adaptive reasoning + effort dial
competitors.context_memory: CLAUDE.md tree + @imports + .claude/rules; auto-memory (200 lines/25KB); auto-compaction + /compact + rewind Summarize; Sonnet 5 native 1M context
competitors.tools: Built-in fs/edit/shell/search/git + WebFetch(isolated ctx); read-only Bash allowlist; PowerShell parity
competitors.mcp: Client + host; .mcp.json/managed; stdio/streamable-http/sse/ws; OAuth 2.0; @resources; prompts as commands; tool-search deferral default-on; 10k warn/25k cap spill
competitors.skills_hooks: Skills (SKILL.md progressive disclosure, context:fork); plugins + marketplaces (SHA-pinned, ~11 LSP plugins); hooks (11+ events, JSON-decision, execute on trust)
competitors.subagents_teams: File-defined subagents (isolated ctx, depth 5, 200/session); experimental agent teams (shared task list + mailbox, ~7x tokens, one team/session, no nesting); background agents dashboard
competitors.worktrees: Background sessions auto-isolate in git worktrees, auto commit/push/draft-PR (never main/force-push)
competitors.durability: JSONL transcripts; --continue/--resume picker (worktree/branch/all/PR scope); checkpoints (100, 30-day) + rewind code/conv/both; /branch + --fork-session; supervisor daemon survives sleep/reboot; cloud Routines
competitors.verification: /goal evaluator; deterministic gates via hooks; test/build via bash
competitors.security: Workspace-trust gate (config inert until trust); /sandbox (Seatbelt/bubblewrap, cwd+temp writes, egress default-deny proxy, credential mask); ~84% fewer prompts; auto-mode classifier (~83% catch/0.4% FP/17% FN); srt open-source; managed lockdown keys
competitors.local_support: NONE first-class; cloud-only Anthropic models; users point it at local models as a workaround
competitors.cost_model: Metered: 5-hour window + weekly caps (all-models + Opus-specific); Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise seats; /usage local-session-only
competitors.observability: /usage attribution, /context grid, transcript viewer, status line JSON
competitors.notable_2026: Auto mode + sandboxing GA; /goal (v2.1.139); checkpointing/rewind; Fable 5 / Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 5 (1M) / Haiku 4.5 + effort dial (low..max/ultracode); cloud Routines; Remote Control (steer local from web/mobile); desktop redesign; is itself a listed ACP agent in Zed/JetBrains
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Deepest, most polished single-vendor integration of the whole stack; best steerable-autonomy ergonomics; strongest documented security thesis (deterministic > probabilistic)
competitors.local_first_opening: It is DELIBERATELY cloud-only, metered, egress-required, and remotely mutable - every one of those is a HIDE structural wedge

competitors.name: Cursor
competitors.ide_ux: Full GUI IDE (VS Code fork); integrated browser + Design Mode (shipped 3.0, Apr 2 2026 [verifier-corrected from 3.5])
competitors.agent_loop: Agent mode; per-model harness profiles; measures Keep Rate; tool errors persist in context
competitors.subagents_teams: /best-of-n across parallel worktrees (multi-model tournament, pick winner)
competitors.durability: Cloud/background agents in isolated remote workspaces
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: First-class best-of-N worktree tournament; integrated browser + UI Design Mode annotation; polished GUI
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE can offer best-of-N via warm-state forks at near-zero marginal cost (Cursor pays per run + cloud VM); local browser verification with no screenshot upload

competitors.name: OpenAI Codex CLI + Symphony
competitors.agent_loop: Codex loop (output->tool->observe->context mgmt); Codex-CLI exposes codex()/codex-reply() MCP tools + native subagents [verifier: OpenAI-documented]
competitors.subagents_teams: Symphony: board-as-control-plane (Linear/issues as continuous dispatch queue; agents self-assign, open PRs); self-reported '500% more landed PRs' [vendor claim]
competitors.durability: Per-task workspaces, retries, stall detection, resumable
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE can run a self-dispatching queue overnight at fixed hardware cost (no unbounded cloud spend); warm forks keep each ticket resident

competitors.name: Zed + ACP / JetBrains
competitors.ide_ux: Zed agent panel; ACP (Agent Client Protocol): editor owns thread UI/history, agent owns runtime/model/tools; agent registry
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Neutral agent-hosting protocol - ANY agent (including a local one) plugs into a real IDE; Claude Code is itself a listed ACP agent [verifier-added]
competitors.local_first_opening: STRONGEST interop lever: HIDE ships an ACP server to appear natively in Zed/JetBrains alongside Claude Code/Codex/Gemini, with no auth/subscription handshake and no egress; warm-state resume beats cloud agents' re-prefill

competitors.name: GitHub Copilot / VS Code agent mode
competitors.ide_ux: Native VS Code agent mode; project instructions, skills, hooks, MCP, specialized agents, policy gates
competitors.context_memory: Copilot Memory: repo facts with source citations, re-verified against current branch before use [strong pattern]
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE's outcome-governed memory can do local revalidation with no code egress

competitors.name: Google Antigravity
competitors.verification: Real-Chrome subagent: drives the actual app, clicks/fills/screenshots, sees failures, fixes; artifacts (task lists, plans, screenshots, browser recordings) as the review surface with inline annotation
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Artifact-based review + real-browser self-verification; Managed Agents API
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE runs headless-browser verification locally (no screenshot upload, no metered re-run); capsules re-enter exact app state after each fix

competitors.name: Windsurf / Devin (Cognition)
competitors.security: Devin Local: rewritten in Rust, OS-level filesystem isolation + network allow/deny in the runtime [verifier: DOCUMENTED via Devin changelog]
competitors.local_support: Devin Local runs on-machine; SWE-1.6 model
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Repo-committed workflows/recipes; OS sandbox baked into runtime; local desktop
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE defaults network to DENY (no egress needed for inference) - strictly safer than agents that must reach a model API

competitors.name: Aider
competitors.agent_loop: Architect/editor two-model split (strong reasoner plans, cheap/fast model emits diff; ~30-50% cheaper, better multi-file edits)
competitors.context_memory: Graph-ranked RepoMap under a token budget (~1K default) - a serious deterministic baseline
competitors.local_support: First-class with Ollama; model-agnostic; works offline
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Deterministic RepoMap baseline; architect/editor split; watch-mode AI comments
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE runs both roles locally with warm state shared (editor reads planner's capsule, no re-send as text); RepoMap is a baseline HIDE's index must beat

competitors.name: mini-SWE-agent
competitors.agent_loop: ~100 lines, bash-only, linear history, no tool-calling requirement; still >74% SWE-bench
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Radical minimalism + full auditability; a control for whether harness complexity actually helps
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE offers a transparent 'thin' mode; capsules enable deterministic replay + fork-from-step debugging

competitors.name: Qwen Code
competitors.agent_loop: qwen serve: persistent daemon exposing a shared session over HTTP+SSE that many clients (CLI/desktop/IDE/bots) attach to
competitors.what_it_does_that_others_dont: Shared session daemon (warm agent state over HTTP) + a strong local coder architecture
competitors.local_first_opening: This IS Hawking's 'pass state not text' thesis, but Hawking can go further: the daemon holds resident KV/state capsules so attach/fork is a pointer copy, not a re-prefill; Qwen3-Coder-Next is HIDE's first architecture-fit local coder target

competitors.name: Gemini CLI
competitors.subagents_teams: Subagents as .md + YAML frontmatter; @agent delegation syntax
competitors.local_first_opening: HIDE reads the same markdown+frontmatter conventions verbatim (interop, zero migration friction)

differentiators_claude_code_lacks: genuinely local/offline operation (Aider+Ollama, Qwen Code, mini-SWE-agent)
field_synthesis.hide_strategic_positioning: Reproduce Claude Code's polished steerable-autonomy loop, read its config verbatim, then win on the six things it structurally cannot do locally (no meter, no egress, no remote regression, warm-state forks/best-of-N, resident daemon, ACP-native local hosting)
